_comment: Candor tokenizer alignment benchmark. Each group tracks a design question. Comparisons show Verification Form vs Agent Form equivalents. Re-run when a major model ships.

groups.name: core_keywords
groups.description: Every keyword must be 1 token. If any goes multi-token in a future model, it becomes a candidate for renaming or Agent Form aliasing.

groups.name: numeric_types
groups.description: All integer/float types split as letter+number (2 tokens). This is fundamental BPE behavior — unlikely to change. Confirms that Agent Form type aliases (int, float, byte) save 1 token each.

groups.name: agent_form_type_aliases
groups.description: Agent Form candidates for single-token type aliases. These are 1-token words that could alias to their 2-token Candor equivalents in Agent Form only.

groups.name: operators
groups.description: Operators used in Candor syntax. 1-token operators are ideal. |> is 2 tokens — expected but still cheaper than nested calls. ?| is 2 tokens (error-transform variant of ?).

groups.name: effect_annotations
groups.description: Current Verification Form effect syntax vs proposed Agent Form shorthands. The savings are large: 4 tokens vs 1 token per effect declaration.

comparisons.label: pure (already optimal — no Agent Form alias needed)

groups.name: result_type_expressions
groups.description: Verification Form result<T,str> vs Agent Form ?T shorthand. The savings are 50-64% per return type annotation.

groups.name: full_return_signatures
groups.description: Complete return type + annotation fragments — the most common signature suffix patterns. Combines result type + effect savings.

comparisons.label: effectful result fn (common: load, run, write)

groups.name: identifier_splitting
groups.description: How snake_case function names split. Always: word + underscore + word = 3 tokens minimum. CamelCase struct names vary: short names (Row, Config) are 1 token; compound names (LogEntry) are 2. This is intrinsic to BPE and unlikely to improve.

groups.name: collection_types
groups.description: Container types with type parameters. Each angle bracket and comma is 1 token. The type parameter itself adds its token count.

groups.name: refmut_agent_form
groups.description: refmut is 3 tokens — the only core keyword that fails the 1-token test. Agent Form alias: mut<T> = 4 tokens vs refmut<T> = 6 tokens (33% savings). Re-check on each new model to see if this changes.

groups.name: future_keywords
groups.description: Keywords being considered for M14 and beyond. All should be 1 token before committing to them as language keywords.

groups.name: question_mark_propagation
groups.description: ? operator vs full match error-propagation boilerplate. The highest token-density operator in the language: 1 token replaces 15-25 tokens of routing noise with zero semantic loss.

comparisons.label: single propagation site
comparisons.verification: match parse(s) { ok(v) => v   err(e) => return err(e) }

comparisons.label: propagation with named-type result
comparisons.verification: match load_config(path) { ok(v) => v   err(e) => return err(e) }

comparisons.label: 3 propagation sites in one function (realistic IO fn body)
comparisons.verification: match open(path) { ok(v) => v   err(e) => return err(e) } match read(f) { ok(v) => v   err(e) => return err(e) } match parse(s) { ok(v) => v   err(e) => return err(e) }

comparisons.label: 5 propagation sites (complex pipeline fn)
comparisons.verification: match open(path) { ok(v) => v   err(e) => return err(e) } match read(f) { ok(v) => v   err(e) => return err(e) } match parse(s) { ok(v) => v   err(e) => return err(e) } match validate(row) { ok(v) => v   err(e) => return err(e) } match compute(row) { ok(v) => v   err(e) => return err(e) }

groups.name: pipe_operator
groups.description: |> pipeline vs nested function calls. Token-neutral for 1-token fn names; saves tokens when names are snake_case (3 tokens each). Primary AI value: linear left-to-right reasoning vs depth-tracking for nested parens.

comparisons.label: 5-step pipeline, snake_case names
comparisons.agent_form: line |> parse_row |> filter_lines |> compute_stats |> format_report |> write_output

groups.name: compound_savings
groups.description: Full function signatures combining multiple M14 features: result type shorthand + ? propagation + |> pipeline. Shows compounding effect on a realistic fn declaration + body fragment.

comparisons.label: fn signature: effectful result fn
comparisons.verification: fn run(path: str) -> result<str, str> effects(io)

comparisons.label: fn body: 2 propagations + 1 pipeline
comparisons.verification: let f = match open(path) { ok(v) => v   err(e) => return err(e) } let s = match read(f) { ok(v) => v   err(e) => return err(e) } let r = filter_lines(parse_row(s))
comparisons.agent_form: let f = open(path)? let s = read(f)? let r = s |> parse_row |> filter_lines

comparisons.label: complete short fn (signature + 3-propagation body)
comparisons.verification: fn process(path: str) -> result<str, str> effects(io) { let f = match open(path) { ok(v) => v   err(e) => return err(e) } let s = match read(f) { ok(v) => v   err(e) => return err(e) } let r = match parse(s) { ok(v) => v   err(e) => return err(e) } return ok(r) }
comparisons.agent_form: fn process(path: str) -> ?str io { let f = open(path)? let s = read(f)? let r = parse(s)? return ok(r) }
